evals.description: Should trigger when the user asks to document a feature
evals.prompt: Using absolute-documentations, write docs for our new webhooks feature.

evals.description: Should trigger for repo developer docs like a README
evals.prompt: Write a README for this repo using absolute-documentations.

evals.description: Should enter AUDIT mode when the user says their docs are a mess
evals.prompt: Our docs are a mess, audit them with absolute-documentations.

evals.description: Should classify a request into the correct Diátaxis quadrant before writing
evals.prompt: Using absolute-documentations: I want a doc that helps an experienced user rotate their API keys without downtime. What kind of document is this and why?

evals.description: Should propose an outline and stop for approval before writing prose
evals.prompt: Using absolute-documentations, write a tutorial for getting started with our CLI. Go.

evals.description: Should answer intake questions from the repo before asking the user
evals.prompt: Using absolute-documentations, document our config options. What do you need from me?

evals.description: Should detect the docs stack from marker files and follow its conventions
evals.prompt: Using absolute-documentations: my repo has a docusaurus.config.ts. How will you format callouts and tabs in the docs you write?

evals.description: Should classify, verify, and rewrite an existing doc in IMPROVE mode
evals.prompt: Using absolute-documentations, improve this README - it's 800 lines and covers installation, full API docs, architecture and an FAQ.

evals.description: Should refuse to mix quadrants and propose linking instead
evals.prompt: Using absolute-documentations, write a tutorial that also fully explains our event-sourcing architecture and documents every config option along the way.

evals.description: Should pick a single path in tutorials rather than offering alternatives
evals.prompt: Using absolute-documentations, in the getting-started tutorial, should we show npm, pnpm, yarn and bun install commands as equal options?

evals.description: Should keep reference neutral and move recommendations to how-to guides
evals.prompt: Using absolute-documentations, in the config reference table, add a note saying we recommend everyone set maxRetries to 10 because it's the best value.

evals.description: Should refuse to document a feature it cannot find in the code
evals.prompt: Using absolute-documentations, write reference docs for our --turbo-cache CLI flag. (Assume you searched the codebase and no such flag exists.)

evals.description: Should source code snippets and defaults from the codebase, not memory
evals.prompt: Using absolute-documentations, you remember roughly how our client library works from similar libraries. Just write the API examples from memory to save time?

evals.description: Should load the tutorials reference file when writing a tutorial
evals.prompt: Using absolute-documentations, walk me through your full playbook for writing a getting-started tutorial.
